title: Mineralogy, Crystallography and Petrography

prerequisites.text: GEO1009F and GEO1006S; CEM1000W or equivalent.

outline: This course covers the fundamentals of physical and chemical mineralogy and its application to igneous and metamorphic rocks as a basis for senior courses in petrology. Inter-related sections include: crystallography and crystal optics (including a brief introduction to X-ray crystallography); mineralogy (chemical, physical and optical properties of selected groups of rock-forming minerals, and theory and practice of identifying minerals by polarising microscope); phase diagrams (interpretation of simple phase diagrams relevant to igneous and metamorphic rocks); classification and petrography of igneous rocks (physical processes of magma differentiation and the relationship between chemical composition and modal mineralogy); classification and petrography of metamorphic rocks (types of metamorphism, metamorphic textures and mineral assemblages).
dp_requirements: Attendance at 80% of practicals and an average of 30% in all marked classwork and tests.
assessment: Marked classwork including tests counts 20%; one 2-hour practical examination in June counts 30%; one 2-hour theory examination in June counts 50%. Sub-minima of 40% are required in both practical and theory examination papers.
